items.title: With EU budget battle underway, Commission proposes to increase own resources
items.description: EU leaders are meeting in Brussels to talk about the bloc's next seven-year budget, set to go on for the next six months. The European Commission proposed last July a two-trillion-euro seven-year spending plan for the 27-country bloc -- which so-called frugal countries immediately made clear was too high. In the opposing camp, France, long a supporter of common debt, is pushing for ambitious spending as the bloc strives for independence in the technology and defence fields. In the middle, the Commission suggests to develop its own resources. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating tells us more.

items.title: Obama's presidential centre opens in Chicago with call to defend democracy
items.description: The Obama museum and civic center has been inaugurated in Chicago. It tells the story and cements the legacy of the former president, championning the principles of democracy. All former US presidents attended the event, as well as several celebrities. The nearly 20-acre (8-hectare) campus includes a museum featuring a life-sized replica of the Oval Office, a garden designed by former first lady Michelle Obama complete with lettuce and strawberry plants, a professional-grade basketball court, a picnic area with grills and a new branch of the Chicago Public Library. Visitors can expus erience high-tech and hands-on exhibits spanning the campaigns, key moments of Obama’s presidency and life at the White House.

New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
items.description: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

items.title: Two men jailed for arson attacks on property linked to UK prime minister
items.description: The Ukrainian-born men with alleged links to Russia targeted property associated with Keir Starmer in May 2025.

items.title: Court warns former president Cristina Fernández de Kirchner over supporters' rallies outside home
items.description: Court warns former president over supporters' rallies outside her home and says she must comply with conditions of house arrest or face seeing restrictions tightened. Leer más

items.title: U.S. strike on an alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
items.description: The latest attack brings the number of people who have been killed in boat strikes by the U.S. military to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting those it calls "narcoterrorists" in early September.

items.title: How armed groups in Colombia are using TikTok to recruit young people
items.description: As Colombia’s conflict has intensified in recent years, armed groups have flooded social media like TikTok with videos aimed at getting new recruits – particularly young people. The FRANCE 24 Observers team investigated the inner workings of this new recruitment method.

items.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
items.description: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

items.title: West Asia highlights: Oil down 2% after U.S., Iran sign ceasefire agreement
items.description: The U.S. on Thursday lifted its blockade of Iran, and oil tankers began freely moving through the Strait of Hormuz after months of being unable to use the critical channel, as the tentative agreement to end the war took effect

items.title: Argentina launch bid for back-to-back title in Messi's sixth World Cup
items.description: Algeria will be Argentina's first hurdle in Group J, while Austria and Jordan await in Dallas on June 22 and June 27. Leer más

items.title: Kenya intensifies screening and awareness for truck drivers over Ebola fears
items.description: Kenya's health authorities have intensified health screening for truck drivers whose movements span thousands of kilometres across multiple countries, as East Africa remains on heightened alert following the latest outbreak of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

items.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
items.description: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

items.title: Gold Coast council reconsiders return of ocean pools
items.description: Its been decades since ocean pools had a presence along the Gold Coast shoreline. Now, council is considering whether they could make a comeback after strong community interest.

items.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
items.description: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.

items.title: De Minaur and Hijikata bundled out of Queen's Club quarterfinals
items.description: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata both go down in the Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals as they prepare for Wimbledon.

items.title: World Cup dreams come true for young German footballers
items.description: Three young US college soccer players from Germany have had the opportunity to meet some of their World Cup heroes, as the men's natonal team's base camp is just around the corner.

items.title: Messi family seeks privacy with statement on health of player’s father

items.title: Baby twins Artie and Jack were born deaf. Watch them hear their mum's voice
items.description: An Australian-first eight hour surgical feat had one main purpose — for Artie and Jack to hear their mum's voice at the same time.

items.title: Zombie director Yeon Sang-ho says AI wants to 'fold' us into one

items.title: What you need to know about the preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement signed by Trump

items.title: What's in the US-Iran agreement?
items.description: The 14-paragraph memo includes an end to fighting, an agreement that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon, and a $300bn redevelopment package for Iran.

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.description: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones were launched towards an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: The women planting the seeds of a flower farm movement
items.description: Women looking for a new career or to invest in themselves are turning to micro flower farms, but pricing their blooms against industry giants can be challenging.

items.title: Times Square gunfire sends bystanders scrambling to safety
items.description: The shooting took place at around 3:40 pm, which was after the parade for the NBA champion New York Knicks filled the streets of lower Manhattan.

items.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
items.description: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.description: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: Israel must stop hostilities in Lebanon, U.S. must pressure Israel, French Minister says
items.description: Israel’s military said on Friday (June 19) its forces struck targets throughout southern Lebanon overnight as Hezbollah reported intense fighting in the area, threatening the nascent agreement between Iran and the United States to end their war.

items.title: Treating energy crisis with fossil fuels is like giving a diabetic sugar: EU Commissioner Jorgensen
items.description: EU leaders have cautiously welcomed Donald Trump's signing of an interim agreement with Iran, after nearly four months of war in the Middle East. But it is only a Memorandum of Understanding, and Europeans know the US president too well to celebrate prematurely. The conflict has led to the most severe disruption in energy supplies in decades, and the EU has scrambled to contain the costs.

items.title: U.S.-Iran war: Timeline of key events
items.description: The United States and Iran have reached an interim deal aimed at ending their war and reopening the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
items.description: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

items.title: Italy: Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo
items.description: Donald Trump claimed that Giorgia Meloni had 'begged' for a photo with him during the recent G7 summit. He added that he wasn’t obliged to do it but that he felt sorry for her and agreed.

items.title: 'Everything's just so wrong': Inside the country town that turned to One Nation
items.description: In just a few short weeks, a small country town became One Nation heartland. It is a blueprint for how the party can turn Australia orange.

items.title: 'Major discovery': France's National Library brings forgotten Mozart manuscript back to life
items.description: A long-forgotten manuscript by composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart will be brought to life this weekend in Paris. The newly rediscovered work – composed in 1778 when the Austrian prodigy was just 22 – will be performed in public for the first time ever at France's National Library.

items.title: Tireless human rights activist Taty Almeida, founding member of Madres de Plaza de Mayo, dies at 95
items.description: Teacher, iconic human rights activist, president of Madres de Plaza de Mayo Línea Fundadora and campaigner for the disappeared and downtrodden. Taty Almeida, who has died at the age of 95, would not be silenced in her tireless search for memory, truth and justice and the knowledge over what happened to her missing son Alejandro. Leer más
